channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Accused Umar Hayat Denies Confession in Sana Yousaf Murder Case, Alleges Police Misconduct
item.description: In a major turn in the case of 17-year-old social media influencer Sana Yousaf, accused Umar Hayat has withdrawn his earlier confession and described the murder as a 'blind killing' in Islamabad court...

item.title: Toronto Hosts First World Symposium Addressing Antizionism and Antisemitism
item.description: Toronto recently hosted the first World Symposium Against Antizionism, marking a significant step in confronting modern antisemitism in Canada. The event brought together academics, journalists, lawye...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the Beach Boys’ Pet Sounds Album Cover at San Diego Zoo
item.description: The iconic album 'Pet Sounds' by the Beach Boys, released on May 16, 1966, was largely composed, arranged, and produced by Brian Wilson with contributions from lyricist Tony Asher. Recorded between Ja...

item.title: Pete Hegseth Supports Trump-Endorsed Candidate Amid Speculation of 2028 Presidential Run
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s recent appearance on the campaign trail in Kentucky has sparked speculation about a possible 2028 presidential run. Hegseth attended an event supporting Ed Gallrein, a...

item.title: Post-Budget Polls Show Mixed Public Opinion on Economic Measures
item.description: The latest post-budget polling from Newspoll and Resolve highlights a divided response to Treasurer Jim Chalmers' fifth federal budget. Newspoll data, as reported by The Australian, suggests that this...

item.title: Eurovision performance sparks debate on UK's relationship with the EU
item.description: Richard Littlejohn reflects on the UK's recent Eurovision performance, using it as a metaphor to criticise the idea of rejoining the European Union. The UK entry, Look Mum No Computer, came last and w...

item.title: A Young Man’s Ambition and the Twist of Fate in Rural India
item.description: The story, excerpted from 'Kissagram: A Novella and Other Stories' by Prabhat Ranjan and translated by Satyadeep Chhetri, follows a young man named Pikku from a small town in Bihar. He aspires to join...

item.title: World Gymnastics Allows Belarusian and Russian Gymnasts to Compete Again
item.description: World Gymnastics don remove all the restrictions wey dem put for Belarusian and Russian gymnasts since the Ukraine war start for 2022. Dem executive committee meet for Sharm El-Sheikh last weekend and...

item.title: Liberal Party Faces Membership Exodus to One Nation, Eyes Return to Centrist Policies
item.description: The Liberal Party is experiencing a notable exodus of members to Pauline Hanson's One Nation, signalling internal instability and dissatisfaction with current leadership. Recently, figures such as for...

item.title: BoT and HESLB start joint financial literacy programme for higher education students
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) and the Higher Education Students’ Loans Board (HESLB) have launched a joint programme to give financial education to students in higher learning institutions across Tanzani...

item.title: Security Deployed on Thika Road and Kiambu During Nationwide Fuel Price Strike
item.description: On Monday, parts of Kiambu County and Thika Road experienced heavy security deployment as the nationwide PSV strike over soaring fuel prices turned violent. Protesters vandalised vehicles, set tyres o...

item.title: Defence Minister Asif warns India against future military aggression
item.description: On Monday, Defence Minister Khawaja Asif strongly warned that India would face severe consequences if it attempted any future misadventure against Pakistan. Speaking on Geo News' programme 'Capital Ta...

item.title: Spanish High Court Clears Shakira of 2011 Tax Fraud Allegations
item.description: Colombian singer Shakira has been acquitted of tax fraud by Spain's High Court, which overturned a €55-million fine imposed by the Spanish tax agency in 2021. The ruling was issued after Shakira appea...

item.title: Mali’s military leader centralises power amid ongoing insecurity
item.description: For Mali, wahala never end since the military waka enter power for 2020. General Assimi Goïta, the head honcho of the junta, don decide say him go also handle the defence ministry after General Sadio ...

item.title: Rodrigo Koraicho Captures the Vibrant Humanity of Miami Beach Through Street Photography
item.description: Brazilian photographer Rodrigo Koraicho has spent years walking along Miami Beach, capturing the ebb and flow of human life under the Florida sun. In his project, 'Beach Epiphany,' he explores the bea...

item.title: Controversy Over DOJ $1.776 Billion Fund to Settle Trump Allies' Claims
item.description: The Department of Justice has announced a $1.776 billion fund intended to settle claims from former President Donald Trump, his family, and associates who argue they were politically targeted by previ...

item.title: Incumbent Borno Senators Reaffirmed Unopposed in APC Primaries
item.description: The three sitting senators from Borno State, namely Ali Ndume (Borno South), Mohammed Monguno (Borno North), and Kala Shehu Lawan, SAN, (Borno Central) have all returned unopposed in the APC primaries...

item.title: Cruise Ship Arrives in Rotterdam After Hantavirus Outbreak, Crew Quarantined
item.description: A cruise ship operated by Dutch company Oceanwide Expeditions concluded its voyage in Rotterdam on Monday after a hantavirus outbreak sparked international concern. The vessel, which began its journey...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for 19 May 2026
item.description: The Age offers its readers a daily Superquiz along with a Mini Crossword designed for those who enjoy bite-sized puzzle challenges. The Mini Crossword features ten clues across twenty-five squares, pr...

item.title: Delhi High Court refuses to allow Vinesh Phogat in Asian Games trials; WFI policies upheld
item.description: On May 18, 2026, the Delhi High Court denied wrestler Vinesh Phogat’s request for interim relief to participate in the 2026 Asian Games selection trials scheduled on May 30 and 31. Justice Purushaindr...
